checkpoint_gates.authority_safety_posture: Authority rebuild and binding revalidation are distinct mandatory gates.
checkpoint_gates.operator_visible_effect: Restore operators can see the specific missing checkpoint gate instead of a generic not-ready badge.
checkpoint_gates.recovery_posture: Verified checkpoint and reopen claims fail closed until every required gate is bound explicitly.
checkpoint_gates.state_or_outcome: Checkpoint cannot claim VERIFIED without bound restore drill evidence and verification basis.
checkpoint_gates.trigger_or_entry_condition: A checkpoint claims VERIFIED posture or an environment claims READY_FOR_REOPEN.

privacy_reconciliation_states.authority_safety_posture: Authority ambiguity blocks reopen instead of being resolved by deletion or omission.
privacy_reconciliation_states.operator_visible_effect: Operators can see whether restore is reconciled, compensating re-erasure is in progress, or review is blocked by holds or proof preservation.
privacy_reconciliation_states.recovery_posture: Restore privacy posture remains durable and grouped with limitation and audit continuity evidence.
privacy_reconciliation_states.trigger_or_entry_condition: A restore privacy reconciliation contract evaluates resurrected data, limitations, and hold blockers.

recovery_policies.authority_safety_posture: Queue rebuild cannot authorize duplicate authority mutation or replay without lineage proof.
recovery_policies.operator_visible_effect: Recovery runbooks can rebuild work queues deterministically from durable artifacts.
recovery_policies.recovery_posture: Queue state is rebuildable projection, not legal source of record.
recovery_policies.trigger_or_entry_condition: Queues or projections must be reconstructed after restore, failover, or broker loss.

recovery_policies.authority_safety_posture: No-blind-resend and binding-lineage validation survive restore, reclaim, and fail-forward pressure.
recovery_policies.operator_visible_effect: Authority recovery tools can point to exact persisted receipts and reconciliation packets.
recovery_policies.recovery_posture: Authority recovery rebuilds outstanding transmit and reconciliation work from durable truth instead of broker replay.
recovery_policies.trigger_or_entry_condition: Outstanding authority-integrated work is resumed after restore or failover.

recovery_policies.authority_safety_posture: Authority ambiguity or proof-preservation conflicts block reopen instead of being hidden.
recovery_policies.operator_visible_effect: Restore dashboards surface blocked legal-hold, proof-preservation, and authority-ambiguity review states.
recovery_policies.recovery_posture: Privacy reconciliation is part of reopen law, not a later cleanup queue.
recovery_policies.trigger_or_entry_condition: Restore completed for an environment containing retained or previously erased restricted data.

recovery_tier_mappings.authority_safety_posture: Authority-integrated control-plane truth may not serialize a weaker tier than the mapping allows.
recovery_tier_mappings.operator_visible_effect: SRE and release teams can prove the expected RPO and RTO for each protected workload class.
recovery_tier_mappings.recovery_posture: Tier mapping is shared across RecoveryCheckpoint and DeploymentRelease artifacts.
recovery_tier_mappings.state_or_outcome: CONTROL_PLANE_LEGAL_TRUTH maps to TIER_0_CONTROL_PLANE / RPO_15M / RTO_60M.
recovery_tier_mappings.trigger_or_entry_condition: A checkpoint or deployment release binds recovery governance.

recovery_tier_mappings.authority_safety_posture: Authority-integrated control-plane truth may not serialize a weaker tier than the mapping allows.
recovery_tier_mappings.operator_visible_effect: SRE and release teams can prove the expected RPO and RTO for each protected workload class.
recovery_tier_mappings.recovery_posture: Tier mapping is shared across RecoveryCheckpoint and DeploymentRelease artifacts.
recovery_tier_mappings.state_or_outcome: REBUILDABLE_PROJECTION maps to TIER_1_REBUILDABLE / RPO_4H / RTO_4H.
recovery_tier_mappings.trigger_or_entry_condition: A checkpoint or deployment release binds recovery governance.

recovery_tier_mappings.authority_safety_posture: Authority-integrated control-plane truth may not serialize a weaker tier than the mapping allows.
recovery_tier_mappings.operator_visible_effect: SRE and release teams can prove the expected RPO and RTO for each protected workload class.
recovery_tier_mappings.recovery_posture: Tier mapping is shared across RecoveryCheckpoint and DeploymentRelease artifacts.
recovery_tier_mappings.state_or_outcome: DISPOSABLE_RUNTIME_CACHE maps to TIER_2_DISPOSABLE / RPO_BEST_EFFORT / RTO_24H.
recovery_tier_mappings.trigger_or_entry_condition: A checkpoint or deployment release binds recovery governance.

reopen_readiness_states.authority_safety_posture: Authority ambiguity review and binding revalidation remain explicit reopen blockers.
reopen_readiness_states.operator_visible_effect: Restore dashboards can show the exact blocker, quarantine, expiry, or ready-for-reopen state.
reopen_readiness_states.recovery_posture: Readiness state is explicit and typed; reopen never happens through generic success inference.
reopen_readiness_states.trigger_or_entry_condition: RecoveryCheckpoint serializes its reopen readiness state after restore evaluation.

rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can see whether restore is reconciled, compensating re-erasure is in progress, or review is blocked by holds or proof preservation.
rows.recovery_posture: Restore privacy posture remains durable and grouped with limitation and audit continuity evidence.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Queue rebuild cannot authorize duplicate authority mutation or replay without lineage proof.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Recovery runbooks can rebuild work queues deterministically from durable artifacts.
rows.recovery_posture: Queue state is rebuildable projection, not legal source of record.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Queues or projections must be reconstructed after restore, failover, or broker loss.

rows.authority_safety_posture: No-blind-resend and binding-lineage validation survive restore, reclaim, and fail-forward pressure.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Authority recovery tools can point to exact persisted receipts and reconciliation packets.
rows.recovery_posture: Authority recovery rebuilds outstanding transmit and reconciliation work from durable truth instead of broker replay.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Outstanding authority-integrated work is resumed after restore or failover.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Authority ambiguity or proof-preservation conflicts block reopen instead of being hidden.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Restore dashboards surface blocked legal-hold, proof-preservation, and authority-ambiguity review states.
rows.recovery_posture: Privacy reconciliation is part of reopen law, not a later cleanup queue.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Restore completed for an environment containing retained or previously erased restricted data.
